metadata.title: The Measurement We Have vs. The Measurement We Need

tags: The Measurement We Have vs. The Measurem
metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Nik Bear Brown — Founder, Humanitarians AI. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Stable SEO rankings and converting ad spend can hide a real gap in how AI assistants see you. A laptop buyer needing GPU power for video editing may never surface your brand despite good SEO. LLM-driven traffic arrives pre-qualified, converting 20 to 30 percent higher. Those visitors also bounce about 45 percent less, signaling intent the old metrics miss.

artifactLines: Stable SEO rankings and converting ad spend can hide a real gap in how AI assistants see you.
A laptop buyer needing GPU power for video editing may never surface your brand despite good SEO.
LLM-driven traffic arrives pre-qualified, converting 20 to 30 percent higher.
Those visitors also bounce about 45 percent less, signaling intent the old metrics miss.

beats.narration_text: The laptop-brand example, where the buyer needs GPU power for video editing but your SEO looks fine, is exactly the blind spot I want to probe. I want to see the gap between the measurement we have and the one we need. Help me instrument it. Give me: (1) a definition of LLM visibility versus classic SEO ranking, (2) a way to test whether an assistant recommends my product for a real use case, (3) which of those higher-conversion, lower-bounce signals to track first.

output: I’m not talking about a website outage
But here’s what happened to a SaaS company last quarter
